error.signature: AI tells a tourist that Indonesia (Bali) entry is free visa-on-arrival for everyone

verdict.summary: Indonesia offers Visa on Arrival (and e-VOA online) to many nationalities for tourism - but it is NOT free (around IDR 500,000), is valid 30 days, and can be extended once for another 30 days. Some nationalities are not eligible and must obtain a visa in advance.

dead_ends.action: Assume Indonesia/Bali entry is free and open-ended for all tourists
dead_ends.why_fails: Visa on Arrival costs a fee, lasts 30 days, and only nationals on the eligible list qualify. Treating it as free or unlimited leads to unexpected fees or denied entry.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Bali's popularity makes people assume entry is frictionless and free.

dead_ends.action: Overstay the 30-day VOA assuming a small fine at the airport
dead_ends.why_fails: Overstaying incurs a daily fine (around IDR 1,000,000/day) and longer overstays can mean detention and deportation. Extend before expiry instead.

workarounds.action: Buy the e-VOA online before departure (or VOA on arrival) if eligible, and extend once before it expires if needed.
workarounds.how: Apply for e-VOA via the official immigration portal, or pay VOA at the airport; extend once online or at an immigration office before day 30.

workarounds.action: If your nationality is not VOA-eligible, apply for a visit visa in advance through an Indonesian mission or the official e-visa system.
workarounds.how: Check eligibility on imigrasi.go.id and apply for the appropriate visit visa before travel.
workarounds.tradeoff: Advance visas need more documents and processing time.
